ZLD

Semiconductor UPW Reject Recovery: High-Purity Reuse Opportunities

Home/Blog/Article

UPW reject is a recovery opportunity when quality is segmented

Semiconductor facilities generate substantial reject volumes from ultra-pure water (UPW) systems. Not all reject streams have the same quality, and that difference creates reuse opportunity.

Classify reject into quality bands before deciding treatment depth. Some streams can be reused with minimal polishing, while others need advanced conditioning.

Membrane and ion-exchange combinations should be selected based on ionic profile, silica behavior, and trace contamination limits.

Continuous monitoring is mandatory because process drift can impact sensitive downstream applications quickly.

Where zero-discharge targets apply, combine high-purity reuse with controlled concentration paths for final residuals.

A robust control philosophy and contamination response protocol are as important as equipment selection in high-purity environments.
